items.title: I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: Why energy stocks still look so cheap, even after their big rally this month
items.description: Energy stocks are having a big month as oil prices surge on Iran war fears — but it’s not solely the conflict in the Middle East that makes the sector appear attractive.

items.title: Why Micron and other chip stocks are bouncing back so strongly
items.description: The recent chip sell-off presented a buying opportunity, and open-source AI models should drive more memory demand, analysts say

items.title: Forget Nvidia. These 5 S&P 500 stocks are quietly going all in on AI.
items.description: The “Mag Seven” has become the “Bag Seven.” Sharp tech investors are looking elsewhere.

items.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
items.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

items.title: Why Paramount could win if it loses the Warner Bros. Discovery deal
items.description: States are trying to block Paramount’s mega-merger. Shareholders should root for them.

items.title: Pro stock pickers cannot beat simple math — war in the Middle East is proving it
items.description: Active stock pickers almost always lose against the broad market. They can’t beat simple math.

items.title: Oil prices may fall, but gasoline prices won’t. Look at the trap we’re in.
items.description: The Iran war isn’t causing your energy bill to soar. Blame Wall Street for that.

items.title: Think this stock market is in a bubble? These earnings numbers say otherwise.
items.description: Stay with the AI trade and overweight technology stocks, according to one earnings expert.

items.title: Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?
items.description: “Our daughter is a highly ranked competitive softball pitcher with a 3.94 GPA.”
